title: Ochnella humilis
taxonomy: Ochnella humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large group of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is in the clade Saxifragidae and the subclass Rosidae. The clade Saxifragidae is in the larger clade rosids and the clade eudicots. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships.
uses: Ochnella humilis is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds that have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Ochnella humilis has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential in treating cancer and other diseases. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Ochnella humilis.

intro: Ochnella humilis, also known as the small ochna, is a small, shrub-like plant belonging to the family Ochnaceae. Traditionally, Ochnella humilis has been used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ochnella humilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
